context.story_engine: A high school teacher builds a drug empire, testing how far he'll go for family and control

plotlines.name: Walt: Empire
plotlines.goal: build and control his own drug empire after eliminating Gus
plotlines.obstacle: law enforcement pressure, partner conflicts, cancer return, family resistance

plotlines.name: Hank: Investigation
plotlines.goal: uncover and prosecute Heisenberg's true identity
plotlines.obstacle: personal connection to Walt, lack of official support, limited evidence
plotlines.stakes: career destruction, family betrayal, justice denied

plotlines.name: Jesse: Guilt
plotlines.goal: escape the moral weight of his crimes and find redemption
plotlines.obstacle: Walt's manipulation, blood money, inability to undo the past

plotlines.name: Skyler: Protection
plotlines.goal: protect her children and herself from Walt's criminal world
plotlines.obstacle: Walt's refusal to leave, legal complicity, family loyalty

plotlines.name: Mike: Survival
plotlines.goal: protect Gus's imprisoned associates and secure his own exit
plotlines.obstacle: frozen accounts, DEA pressure, Walt's recklessness
plotlines.stakes: death, betrayal of loyal men, legacy destruction

events.event: Walt, Jesse, and Mike meet at junkyard to discuss laptop threat in APD evidence room
Mike wants to flee but Walt insists he has a plan to destroy the evidence
Walt visits home where backyard is torn apart by investigators
Skyler is terrified when Walt tells her 'I won' about Fring's murder
Walt, Jesse, and Mike rent industrial electromagnet from Old Joe
They mount magnet in truck and drive to APD evidence room exterior wall
Walt and Jesse activate magnet at full power, destroying metal evidence inside
Truck tips over from magnetic force, trio abandons it and escapes
Mike protests they can't confirm laptop destruction, Walt says 'Because I say so'
Police discover evidence room wreckage, investigation pivots to mysterious event
Hank notices broken picture frame revealed hidden Cayman Islands bank account numbers
Saul reveals DEA seized Ted Beneke's assets after Huell and Kuby pressured him
Skyler visits hospitalized Ted who broke his neck fleeing Huell and Kuby
Ted promises terrified Skyler he will say nothing about the money
Walt recovers ricin cigarette from Saul's office that Huell had lifted from Jesse
Walt hides real ricin vial behind electrical outlet plate in bedroom
Walt constructs fake ricin cigarette using salt and plants it in Jesse's Roomba
Jesse finds fake cigarette, breaks down in tears of relief
Jesse calls Walt to apologize for accusing him, Walt comforts him

interactions.description: all storylines explore control through deception — Walt controls through lies, Skyler tries to control damage, Jesse is controlled by Walt's manipulation
Walt's magnet plan inadvertently creates new evidence for Hank's investigation
audience knows Walt is manipulating Jesse with the fake ricin cigarette while Jesse believes Walt's innocence

events.event: Peter Schuler kills himself with a defibrillator to avoid DEA questioning about Madrigal's ties to Fring
Hank and Gomez interrogate Mike about his ties to Fring's operation but lack evidence to hold him
Walt proposes to Mike and Jesse that they restart the meth business as equal partners
Mike flatly refuses Walt's partnership proposal, saying he doesn't trust him
DEA freezes Fring's offshore accounts used for hazard pay to imprisoned associates
Mike visits imprisoned associates and promises money he cannot deliver
Lydia presents Mike with a list of eleven people to kill who could implicate her
Mike refuses to kill for Lydia and considers killing her instead
Dennis contacts Hank considering cooperation now that his hazard pay is gone
Jesse argues to Mike that cooking again can generate cash to pay everyone and keep them quiet
Walt, Jesse, and Mike formally agree to a three-way partnership at Saul's office
Mike demands hazard pay for Fring's men comes off the top, Walt reluctantly agrees
Lydia pays Chris Mara to kill ten witnesses plus Mike
Chris kills Chow and lures Mike to an ambush, but Mike anticipates and kills Chris
Mike spares Lydia because Jesse vouches that her Madrigal connections can supply methylamine

interactions.description: all storylines explore loyalty — Walt demanding it from partners, Mike honoring it to Fring's men, Hank's loyalty to justice threatening the network
Hank interrogates Mike not knowing he's questioning Walt's future partner
Mike's need to pay Fring's men drives him to accept Walt's partnership proposal

events.event: Walt moves back into the White family home over Skyler's barely concealed objections
Saul presents cooking venue options including tortilla and box factories
Mike vetoes each venue for security reasons
Jesse suggests mobile cooking inside fumigation-tented houses using Vamonos Pest
The partners agree to use Vamonos Pest and meet Todd and the crew
The team successfully completes their first mobile cook in a tented house
Mike presents the revenue split with extensive deductions for hazard pay and overhead
Walt expresses displeasure at the modest profit shares after deductions
Walt tells Jesse about Victor's death as a warning about overstepping
Marie visits and pushes Skyler to explain her strange behavior
Walt invites Jesse for beer while Skyler isolates in the bedroom
Jesse tells Walt about breaking up with Andrea to protect her and Brock
Skyler tells Walt she is waiting for his cancer to return
Hank and DEA track Fring's money through Madrigal but face bureaucratic delays

interactions.description: all storylines explore different costs of maintaining control — Walt's empire demands profit margins, Skyler's protection requires emotional endurance, Mike's survival needs financial resources
Walt references Victor's death as a warning to Mike, but Jesse doesn't understand that Walt poisoned Brock, making Jesse's guilt about protecting Andrea tragically ironic
Mike's need to pay hazard pay directly conflicts with Walt's empire-building goals, setting up future confrontation

events.event: Skyler buys a birthday cake and arranges family dinner for Walt's 51st birthday
Walt dominates dinner conversation while Skyler barely functions
Skyler walks fully clothed into the pool and sinks to the bottom
Hank and Marie pull Skyler from the pool
Skyler demands the children leave for their safety, using her breakdown as leverage
Walt refuses to send the children away, arguing no one is in danger
Skyler tells Walt her only weapon is waiting for his cancer to return
Walt agrees to send children to Hank and Marie under cover of Skyler needing rest
Walt and Jesse continue cooking in fumigated houses with Vamonos Pest crew
Lydia meets with Hank at DEA, presenting herself as cooperative Madrigal executive
Mike discovers DEA tracking device on his car
Mike warns Walt and Jesse that pressure is mounting from surveillance
Walt dismisses Mike's concerns, confident in outmaneuvering law enforcement
Walt removes his watch and tells Skyler this is just the beginning

episodes.theme: the illusion of control versus powerlessness

interactions.description: all storylines explore different forms of control — Walt's overconfidence, Skyler's desperate manipulation, Mike's cautious pragmatism
Lydia feeds information to Hank while secretly working for Walt; Hank doesn't know he's being manipulated
Hank and Marie don't know the real reason they're taking the children is Walt's criminal activity

events.event: Lydia informs Walt, Jesse, and Mike about a thousand gallons of methylamine being transported by train
The team devises an elaborate plan to rob the train without detection by replacing methylamine with water
Todd is brought in as a fourth crew member for the heist
Walt, Jesse, and Todd rehearse the train robbery operation
Kuby stalls his truck on the railroad tracks, forcing the train to stop
Walt and Todd connect hoses to the tanker car while Jesse manages the pump system
Engineers radio for a second locomotive to push the stalled truck, creating time pressure
Jesse barely escapes from under the moving tanker as the train accelerates
The crew celebrates successfully stealing all the methylamine they need
Todd spots Drew Sharp, a boy on a dirt bike who has been watching the heist
Todd shoots Drew Sharp dead without hesitation
Jesse screams at Todd in anguished shock over the boy's murder

interactions.description: both storylines explore how the pursuit of a perfect crime leads to moral corruption and unintended consequences
the successful heist immediately transforms into Jesse's moral crisis when Todd kills the witness

events.event: Walt, Jesse, and Todd dissolve Drew Sharp's body and dirt bike in hydrofluoric acid
Jesse is deeply shaken and barely speaks during the cleanup
Mike wants Todd gone as a liability, Walt argues Todd followed orders and they need him
Todd apologizes but shows no real remorse, explaining he acted to protect the operation
Walt and Mike reluctantly keep Todd on with a warning
Jesse tells Walt he wants out of the business entirely, willing to walk away from millions
Walt delivers monologue about being bought out of Gray Matter for a pittance
Walt refuses to let Jesse make the same mistake he made with Gray Matter
Jesse remains unmoved, saying the money means nothing against what they have done
Mike informs Walt and Jesse he also wants out
Mike proposes Declan's offer to buy their methylamine supply for fifteen million
Walt objects because selling the methylamine ends his cooking operation
Walt meets Declan alone in the desert, surrounded by armed men
Walt proposes Declan buy out Mike and Jesse but leave Walt his methylamine portion
Walt forces Declan to acknowledge his reputation by demanding 'Say my name'
Declan concedes and says 'Heisenberg', the deal is struck
Jesse receives five million dollars and walks away
Mike receives five million and prepares to leave Albuquerque
Walt begins cooking with Todd as his new assistant, quality drops without Jesse
Walt persists cooking alone, determined to build his empire

interactions.description: all storylines explore different responses to moral compromise — Walt doubles down on ambition, Jesse seeks escape through conscience, Mike seeks practical exit, Walt's legacy obsession drives his refusal to quit
the methylamine deal forces all three partners to confront their different priorities and ultimately splits them apart

events.event: Walt cooks with Todd as his new assistant, but Todd's product only reaches 74 percent purity
Walt coaches Todd through the cooking process with frustrated patience
Mike instructs Saul's paralegal to continue hazard pay distributions to Fring's nine imprisoned associates
Hank and Gomez discover Dan Wachsberger making suspicious deposits into multiple accounts
DEA arrests Dan Wachsberger and he agrees to cooperate
Mike learns about Dan's arrest and realizes the imprisoned men will flip without hazard payments
Mike arranges to flee immediately and calls Saul to retrieve his go-bag from the airport
Walt volunteers to deliver Mike's go-bag instead of Saul
Walt demands the list of Fring's nine associates from Mike, claiming he needs it to arrange their silence
Mike refuses to give Walt the names and tells him he destroyed Fring's perfect operation with his ego
Walt shoots Mike through the abdomen with Mike's own gun after being refused the names again
Mike drives away wounded, collapses by the riverbank, and dies telling Walt to shut up
Walt realizes he could have gotten the names from Lydia and stands over Mike's body processing the murder

interactions.description: both storylines explore how pride and ego destroy carefully built systems and relationships
the audience knows Walt and Mike are connected to what Hank is investigating, but Hank doesn't
Walt's need for control directly destroys Mike's exit strategy, culminating in murder

events.event: Walt dissolves Mike's body in acid inside a plastic barrel
Walt obtains the list of Fring's nine imprisoned associates from Lydia
Walt visits Jack Welker and commissions the murder of all nine men plus their lawyer
Ten men are stabbed and burned to death across three prisons within two minutes
Hank and the DEA are stunned as their entire case evaporates overnight
Walt and Todd cook batch after batch in fumigated houses over three months
Lydia arranges international distribution to the Czech Republic through Madrigal
Skyler shows Walt the storage unit packed with uncountable money
Skyler asks Walt how much money is enough
Walt visits Jesse and they share an awkward conversation about their old RV
Walt learns from his oncologist that his cancer has returned
Walt tells Skyler he is out of the business
Hank finds the Leaves of Grass book with Gale's inscription to W.W.
Hank flashes back to Walt joking that W.W. could stand for Walter White
Hank sits stunned, finally connecting Walter White to Heisenberg

interactions.description: all storylines deal with endings that aren't really endings — Walt thinks he's finished, but his past catches up
Walt believes he's eliminated all threats and can retire safely, while Hank discovers his true identity
Walt's past actions (the Gale book) directly lead to Hank's discovery

events.event: Flash-forward shows Walt's abandoned house vandalized with 'HEISENBERG' graffiti
Future Walt retrieves ricin vial from his destroyed home
Hank suffers panic attack and crashes SUV after discovering Walt Whitman book
Marie tends to Hank, who lies about having a dizzy spell
Hank secretly re-investigates Heisenberg case in his garage
Hank connects Walt to lab equipment theft, Gale's murder, and Fring's death
Walt works mundane car wash routine with Skyler, pretending normalcy
Lydia begs Walt to resume cooking due to Todd's poor quality product
Walt refuses Lydia and tells her never to return
Jesse attempts to give away blood money to victims' families
Saul intercepts the cash and warns Jesse about distributing drug money
Jesse confronts Saul and Walt, calling the money blood money
Jesse directly asks Walt if he killed Mike
Walt lies to Jesse that Mike is alive and on the run
Walt discovers the Walt Whitman book is missing from bathroom
Walt finds GPS tracker under his car
Walt confronts Hank directly in his garage
Hank punches Walt and calls him a monster
Walt warns Hank his cancer returned and threatens him to tread lightly

episodes.theme: the inevitable collision of truth and lies

interactions.description: all storylines deal with confronting the truth of past actions and their consequences
the audience knows Walt killed Mike while Jesse suspects but Walt denies it
Hank's investigation and Walt's criminal empire finally collide in direct confrontation
flash-forward shows the ultimate destruction of everything Walt built

events.event: Hank returns to work at the DEA and tries to build a case against Walt without revealing his personal connection
Hank calls in Jesse for questioning, but Jesse stonewalls completely and says nothing
Skyler senses danger and drives to the storage unit to load money into her car
Walt races to reach the money first, rents a van and loads the barrels himself
Walt takes the money barrels to the desert near To'hajiilee and buries them
Walt records the coordinates as lottery numbers and tapes the ticket inside the refrigerator
The physical labor aggravates Walt's cancer and he collapses in the bathroom
Walt tells Skyler the money is safe and hidden when he wakes
Marie confronts Skyler at the White house, having learned the truth from Hank
Marie slaps Skyler and tries to take baby Holly, but Hank intervenes
Hank realizes he cannot go to the DEA officially because Walt is his brother-in-law
Walt tells Skyler that Hank has no real evidence
Skyler surprises Walt by siding with him out of self-preservation
Skyler asks Walt if this is over and he assures her it is
Lydia visits Declan's underground meth lab and declares the quality unacceptable
Lydia calls Todd, who arrives with Jack's Aryan Brotherhood crew
Jack's men execute Declan and his entire operation in the buried lab
Todd takes over cooking duties and Lydia secures a new supply chain
Hank decides to approach Jesse independently, believing he's the key to building a case outside the system

episodes.theme: the price of loyalty and self-preservation

interactions.description: all three storylines explore characters forced to choose between loyalty and self-preservation — Hank can't use official channels, Skyler sides with Walt to protect herself, Walt hides the money to preserve his legacy
the audience knows Walt's assurance that 'this is over' is false, while Skyler believes it
Hank's decision to approach Jesse independently sets up their alliance against Walt

events.event: Walt and Skyler film a fake confession video framing Hank as Heisenberg
Walt and Skyler invite Hank and Marie to dinner at a Mexican restaurant
Walt slides the DVD confession across the table to Hank
Hank and Marie watch the fake confession video at home
Marie realizes the medical bill detail gives the lie devastating credibility
Jesse sits in Saul's office ready to disappear with a new identity
Huell pickpockets Jesse's marijuana during the pat-down
Jesse realizes Huell stole his weed and connects it to the ricin cigarette theft
Jesse realizes Walt orchestrated Brock's poisoning to manipulate him
Jesse storms back into Saul's office, beats him, and takes his gun
Jesse breaks into the White house and douses it with gasoline
Walt arrives home to find the living room soaked in gasoline
Walt lies to Skyler and Walter Jr. about a gas pump malfunction
The White family moves to a hotel for safety
Walt meets with Saul and instructs him to find Jesse
Saul suggests Jesse needs to be dealt with permanently
Walt refuses to kill Jesse for now
Hank stops Jesse from burning the White house
Hank brings Jesse to the Schrader home
Jesse agrees to cooperate with Hank against Walt

episodes.theme: the price of loyalty and betrayal

interactions.description: all three storylines explore betrayal — Walt betrays Hank with the fake confession, Jesse feels betrayed by Walt's manipulation, Hank is betrayed by his own vulnerability
the audience knows Walt's gas pump story is a lie while his family believes it
Jesse's realization about Walt's manipulation leads directly to his alliance with Hank

events.event: Walt discovers his living room soaked in gasoline from Jesse's break-in
Walt fabricates a gas pump story to explain the smell to Skyler and Walter Jr.
Skyler confronts Walt at the hotel demanding the truth about Jesse
Skyler coldly suggests Walt should kill Jesse permanently
Saul advises Walt that Jesse must be sent to Belize
Walt refuses Saul's advice, insisting he can talk Jesse down
Hank intercepts Jesse at the White house and talks him out of burning it down
Jesse records his full video testimony detailing two years of crimes with Walt
Gomez expresses skepticism that Jesse's testimony alone will convict Walt
Hank devises a sting operation with Jesse wearing a wire to meet Walt
Jesse objects that Walt is too smart and the meeting is a death trap
Hank privately tells Gomez he doesn't care if Jesse dies at the meeting
Jesse panics at the plaza, spots a stranger, and aborts the meeting
Jesse calls Walt with a threatening message about getting him where he really lives
Walt drives to Jack Welker's compound and asks him to kill Jesse
Jack agrees to kill Jesse but demands Walt cook three more batches with Todd

interactions.description: all storylines reach their moral breaking point — Walt ordering Jesse's death, Skyler embracing murder, Jesse threatening Walt, Hank willing to sacrifice Jesse
the audience knows Hank is using Jesse against Walt while Walt seeks Jesse's death
Jesse's moral crisis becomes Hank's investigative weapon

events.event: Jesse and Hank devise a plan to trap Walt by targeting his buried money
Hank intimidates Huell with a staged photo of Jesse's fake death
Huell confirms he helped Walt load seven barrels of cash into a van
Jesse sends Walt a fake photo of excavated money barrel with threat to burn it
Walt panics at the car wash and races toward the burial site
Walt confesses to prison murders, killing Mike, poisoning Brock, and killing Gus while speeding to the desert
Hank records Walt's full confession over the phone
Walt arrives at the burial site and realizes the photo was fake
Walt calls Jack for backup and gives him the coordinates
Walt sees Hank, Gomez, and Jesse and realizes he's been trapped
Walt urgently calls Jack to cancel the backup request
Hank arrests Walt at gunpoint and reads him his Miranda rights
Hank calls Marie and tells her Walt has been arrested and the nightmare is over
Jack's armed convoy arrives despite Walt's cancellation
Armed standoff develops between Jack's crew and Hank and Gomez

interactions.description: all storylines converge on the moment when Walt's carefully constructed lies finally collapse under the weight of truth
the audience knows Walt is walking into a trap while he believes he's protecting his money
all storylines physically converge at the burial site where Walt's empire, Hank's investigation, and the threat to family safety collide

events.event: Flashback shows Walt and Jesse cooking meth in the RV in the desert years earlier
Gomez lies dead after the firefight at To'hajiilee
Hank crawls wounded behind a vehicle, shot in the leg
Walt begs Jack to spare Hank, offering all eighty million dollars
Hank refuses to beg and tells Walt that Jack made his decision ten minutes ago
Jack shoots Hank in the head, killing him
Walt collapses face-down in the dirt after Hank's death
Jack's men dig up Walt's money barrels using GPS coordinates
Jack's crew takes seven barrels, leaving Walt one at Todd's request
Jesse is found hiding under Walt's car and dragged out
Todd argues Jesse should be kept alive to learn what he told the DEA
Walt tells Jesse he watched Jane die and chose not to intervene
Jesse is taken to Jack's compound and chained in an underground pit
Jesse is forced to cook meth for Todd in captivity
Jack's crew buries Hank and Gomez in the hole where the money was
Walt drives through the desert until his car runs out of fuel
Walt buys a battered truck from a Navajo man with cash
Skyler hears news of a shooting in the desert and suspects the worst
Walt bursts in and orders Skyler and Walter Jr. to pack immediately
Skyler grabs a kitchen knife and slashes at Walt, cutting his hand
Walter Jr. throws himself between his parents and calls 911
Walt grabs baby Holly and runs to his truck
Walt changes Holly's diaper in a fire station restroom while she cries for mama
Walt calls Skyler and performs a vicious tirade to shield her from prosecution
Walt leaves Holly at the fire station entrance with a note
Ed Galbraith picks Walt up in a red minivan for his new identity

interactions.description: all storylines culminate in devastating consequences of past choices — Walt's empire crumbles, Hank dies for his pursuit, Jesse faces ultimate betrayal, Skyler loses her family, Walt's legacy is destroyed
the desert confrontation brings Walt's criminal empire and Hank's investigation to their mutual destruction
Walt's phone call appears cruel but actually protects Skyler, while destroying any remaining family bonds
flashback to the RV cooking scene provides emotional contrast to the present devastation

events.event: Walt steals a Volvo in New Hampshire and drives toward Albuquerque
Walt breaks into Elliott and Gretchen's house and waits in the shadows
Walt threatens Elliott and Gretchen with fake hitmen to force them to give money to Walt Jr.
Badger and Skinny Pete are revealed as the fake hitmen with laser pointers
Walt learns from Badger and Skinny Pete that blue meth is still on the street
Walt realizes Jesse must be alive and still cooking
Walt approaches Lydia and Todd at their coffee meeting claiming to have a new cooking method
Walt slips ricin into Lydia's Stevia packet while she dismisses his offer
Walt retrieves his car with the mounted M60 machine gun
Walt gives Skyler the GPS coordinates of Hank and Gomez's burial site
Walt admits he did everything for himself because he liked it and was good at it
Walt looks at Holly sleeping in her crib one last time

interactions.description: all storylines converge on Walt's final acts of control and his attempt to secure what matters to him before death
the audience knows Walt's elaborate schemes while his victims remain unaware of the true danger
Walt's confession to Skyler serves both his need for truth and her need for legal protection

events.event: Walt drives to Jack's compound with the M60 rigged in his trunk
Jack's men search Walt and bring him inside the clubhouse
Walt claims he wants to discuss a deal with Jack
Jack orders Todd to bring Jesse from the meth lab as proof of enslavement
Todd retrieves Jesse, who is emaciated, scarred, and shackled
Walt tackles Jesse to shield him and activates the M60 with the key fob
The M60 sweeps automatic fire through the clubhouse, killing Jack's men
Jesse strangles Todd to death with his handcuff chain
Walt shoots the wounded Jack mid-sentence when Jack offers to reveal the money location
Walt slides the pistol to Jesse and tells him to kill Walt
Jesse refuses to kill Walt, telling him to do it himself
Jesse escapes in Todd's El Camino, laughing and crying as he drives away
Walt tells Lydia over the phone that she ingested ricin and will die
Walt walks through the meth lab and leaves a bloody handprint on equipment
Walt collapses and dies on the lab floor as police arrive

interactions.description: all three storylines converge on the theme of facing consequences and choosing how to end one's story
Walt's final plan simultaneously destroys his enemies, frees Jesse, and completes his transformation

patches.reason: Hank's investigation storyline has no events in the finale - it was resolved in previous episodes
Skyler's protection storyline has no events in the finale - it was resolved in previous episodes
Mike's survival storyline has no events in the finale - it was resolved when Mike was killed
